text: Several peptide hormones are α-helical upon receptor binding, which demands sensitive detection for pathophysiological diagnosis. Designing high affinity binding for folded proteins is now common place in de novo design, but designing binding to short, flexible peptides that only contain random coils connected by helical motifs remain a significant challenge. We here describe methods to overcome the latter challenge through employing parametric modeling and deep learning. We applied an extension of RFdiffusion (RFdiffusionPRO) to flexible targets that combines the partial diffusion method (PDM) comprising successively noising and denoising the molecule, to design picomolar affinity binders to a few examples of these helical motifs both from the optimization of known binders as well as from pure de novo design originating from random noise, requiring no further maturation. We show that the designs facilitated enrichment and sensitive detection of parathyroid hormone (PTH) and glucagon by mass spectrometry (MS), while they can also be adapted to be used in bioluminescent protein based protein-protein biosensors. In summary, designs binders to conformationally variable peptides and refines natural as well as synthetic proteins using the partial diffusion method.
